current_savings.prompt: Roughly how much do you have in savings right now?
current_savings.help: Total of checking, savings, cash. Round to the nearest hundred.

current_monthly_take_home.prompt: About how much do you take home each month from work?
current_monthly_take_home.help: After taxes. If it varies, give a typical month. Put 0 if you don't work right now.

current_monthly_bills.prompt: About how much do you spend each month on bills and stuff you have to pay?
current_monthly_bills.help: Phone, subscriptions, gas, food you buy. Skip rent for now — that comes next.

lives_with_parents.prompt: Do you currently live with a parent or family member?

has_car.prompt: Do you have your own car right now?

car_purpose.prompt: If you had a car, what would it unlock? (pick any)

car_max_savings_spend.prompt: What's the most of your savings you'd feel okay spending on a car?
car_max_savings_spend.help: Total — purchase price + tax + first insurance payment.

car_repair_1k_reaction.prompt: If the car needed a $1,000 repair three months after you bought it, how would you feel?

emergency_fund_floor.prompt: What's the lowest you'd let your savings drop before it would feel scary?
emergency_fund_floor.help: Your emotional 'don't go below this' line.

monthly_pressure_comfort.prompt: How much monthly money pressure feels okay to you?

options.label: I want zero stress about money each month
I can handle real pressure if it's worth it
Bring it on — I'll work harder if I have to

current_job_feeling.prompt: How do you feel about your current job?

options.label: I tolerate it for the money

hours_preference.prompt: Right now, do you want to work…

desired_two_year_income.prompt: Two years from now, what monthly income would feel like 'I made it'?
desired_two_year_income.help: Take-home, your number. There's no right answer.

interested_in_training.prompt: Are you open to training, school, or a certificate that could raise your income?

training_modality.prompt: If you did train, what kind sounds most like you?

max_training_months.prompt: How many months of training would you be willing to do?

interests.prompt: What kinds of work or activities feel interesting to you? (a few words is plenty)

input.placeholder: e.g. animals, coding, cars, helping people, building stuff

move_out_urgency.prompt: How much do you want to move out soon?
move_out_urgency.help: 1 = I'm fine where I am. 5 = I need out.

move_out_horizon.prompt: When would you ideally move out?

rent_max_share_of_income.prompt: What's the most of your monthly income you'd spend on rent?
rent_max_share_of_income.help: As a percentage. (Most experts say 30% is the safe ceiling.)

home_emotional_cost.prompt: How does living at home feel right now?
home_emotional_cost.help: Staying home is cheaper financially, but not always emotionally — we'd rather know.

options.label: Peaceful — I have what I need here
Fine — some friction but mostly okay
Tense — there's pressure I'd like to escape
Hard — staying is costing me real wellbeing

productive_hours_per_week.prompt: Outside of work or school, how many hours a week feel actually productive for you?
productive_hours_per_week.help: Not zoned-out tired hours — hours where you can actually do things.

quality_of_time_now.prompt: Right now, how good is the quality of your free time and energy?
quality_of_time_now.help: 1 = drained and scattered. 5 = rested and ready.

top_value.prompt: Of these, which feels most important to you right now?

one_year_proud_of.prompt: A year from now, what would you love to be able to say you did?

input.placeholder: e.g. saved $5k, finished a course, moved out, learned to drive

top_fear.prompt: What's the thing you most want to avoid happening in the next year?

input.placeholder: e.g. broke, stuck, working a job I hate, drifting

steps.title: Where you are now
steps.blurb: Quick snapshot. We're not judging — we're getting honest numbers so the plan is real.

steps.title: About a car
steps.blurb: A car can unlock a lot. It can also drain a lot.

steps.title: Money comfort
steps.blurb: How much pressure feels okay, and where your floor is.
